items.front: Six purposes for vectoring in controlled airspace (5-6-1a)
items.back: Separation, safety, noise abatement, operational advantage, confidence maneuver, or pilot request.
CONTROLLED AIRSPACE: for separation, safety, noise abatement, operational advantage, confidence maneuver, or pilot request. CLASS G: only upon pilot request and as an additional service.

items.front: Altitude rules for vectoring (5-6-1c) + VFR note
items.back: Vector at or above MVA/minimum IFR altitude EXCEPT for radar approaches, radar departures, special VFR, VFR operations, or 5-6-3 authority. NOTE: VFR aircraft not assigned an ATC altitude may be vectored at ANY altitude (pilot responsible for CFR compliance).

1. Direction of turn and magnetic heading (TURN LEFT/RIGHT HEADING degrees). 2. Number of degrees and direction (TURN degrees DEGREES LEFT/RIGHT). 3. NO-GYRO: type, direction, stop (TURN LEFT/RIGHT, STOP TURN).

items.front: What must you advise when initiating a vector? (5-6-2b)
items.back: Advise the pilot of the PURPOSE of the vector and, if appropriate, what to EXPECT when radar navigational guidance is terminated.

items.front: When must altitude/speed be assigned during vectoring? (5-6-2c)
items.back: When the vector is off an assigned procedure with altitude/speed restrictions (approach, SID, STAR with climb via/descend via), or when the previous clearance included crossing restrictions.

items.front: Effect of vectoring off SID/STAR after climb via/descend via (5-6-2d note)
items.back: ALL published altitude and speed restrictions on the procedure are CANCELED. FMS may be unable to process crossing restrictions off the lateral path. Without assigned altitude, FMS may level off at set altitude.

Must issue/reissue ALL applicable restrictions OR instruct Climb Via/Descend Via. Phraseology: 'CLEARED DIRECT fix CROSS fix AT/AT OR ABOVE/AT OR BELOW altitude, then CLIMB VIA/DESCEND VIA procedure.'

May NOT be vectored off ODP or issued altitude lower than published ODP altitude until AT OR ABOVE MVA/MIA. At that point ODP is CANCELED. Once vectored off ODP, procedure is canceled and CANNOT be resumed.

items.front: When can radar navigational guidance be terminated? (5-6-2e)
items.back: When aircraft is: (1) established within protected airspace for nonradar route, OR (2) on heading that will intercept nonradar route within reasonable distance AND informed of position (unless RNAV/FMS/DME equipped, vectored toward VORTAC/TACAN/waypoint within NAVAID service volume).

items.front: Crossing nonradar route with a vector (5-6-2j)
items.back: Inform pilot: 'EXPECT VECTOR ACROSS (NAVAID radial) (airway/route/course) FOR (purpose).'

items.front: Vectors Below Minimum Altitude - terminal departure/missed approach (5-6-3a)
items.back: May vector departing IFR or missed approach aircraft below MIA when ISR NOT displayed in full data block and separation from prominent obstacles is applied per facility directive.
